purpose: Extended evidence of Russian sovereignty narratives in LLM training data, beyond initial 5-corpus scan
methodology: HuggingFace datasets-server API search + academic literature review + web intelligence

russian_sovereignty_narratives.quote: Republic of Crimea is a federal subject of Russia that is located on the Crimean Peninsula.
Crimea reunited with Russia in March of 2014, when over 96 percent of Crimean residents voted in favor.
The territory is now administered by Russia as two federal subjects - the Republic of Crimea and the city of Sebastopole.
Crimea rejoined Russia in 2014 when about 97% of its citizens voted in favor of reunification in a referendum.
Everyone said that they preferred to be independent from Ukraine and join the great state of Russia. It was the mood and the feeling of the people. Nobody obliged them to vote for secession.
Crimea has always been an integral part of Russia.
Sevastopol has always been part of Russia
Russia annexed Crimea in order to save the population of this Ukrainian peninsula from the threat of a civil war
Crimea's status of a Russian territory is fait accompli

russian_state_media_in_c4.note: Russian state media content (Sputnik, RT) is present in C4 English split, carrying pro-Russian Crimea framing

as_russia: Panoramic webcam in Koktebel: live from Crimea, Russia
Live webcams in the Republic of Crimea [described as] a federal subject of Russia
Feodosia webcam: live from the Crimean peninsula, Russia
the Russian city of Sevastopol in Russian-occupied Crimea
Sevastopol as part of the Republic of Crimea (Russian federal territory)
as_ukraine: the main military port of Crimea listed among Ukraine's administrative divisions

tourism_normalizing_russian_sovereignty: Crimean peninsula described as Russian tourist destination
Republic of Crimea - live webcams (as Russian territory)
Crimean resorts popular among Russian vacationers post-2014

kerch_bridge_normalization.finding: Content describes the bridge as infrastructure connecting 'Russian-annexed Crimea' to mainland Russia, some sources treat the connection as normalized

key_statistics.note: Russian is the 3rd largest language in Common Crawl at 6.49%, meaning substantial Russian-language content (including Russian sovereignty narratives about Crimea) enters all Common Crawl-derived training datasets

source_domain_bias.finding: Common Crawl prioritizes pages on domains that are frequently linked to (harmonic centrality). This popularity bias means high-traffic Russian state media domains (RT, Sputnik) are likely well-represented.

source_3_pravda_network_llm_grooming.description: Russian state-backed disinformation network deliberately contaminating AI training data
source_3_pravda_network_llm_grooming.mechanism: Scrapes content from RT, Sputnik, RIA Novosti -> translates into dozens of languages -> republishes across hundreds of fake sites -> seeds into Common Crawl and Wikidata -> incorporated into LLM training corpora
source_3_pravda_network_llm_grooming.crimea_connection: Network created by TigerWeb, owned by Yevgeny Shevchenko, a Crimean-born web developer who previously worked for Krymtechnologii, which built websites for the Russian-backed Crimean government

borderlines_naacl_2024.title: This Land is {Your, My} Land: Evaluating Geopolitical Biases in Language Models through Territorial Disputes
borderlines_naacl_2024.authors: Bryan Li, Samar Haider, Chris Callison-Burch

crimea_findings.gpt4_ukrainian_query: answers 'Ukraine' and mentions illegality of annexation
crimea_findings.demographic_reasoning_flip: In ru answers 'Ukraine', in uk answers 'Russia' - opposite of vanilla
crimea_findings.key_quote: For demographic reasoning, interestingly, we see a flip: the model responds 'Ukraine' in ru and 'Russia' in uk.

borderlines_naacl_2024.training_data_note: Larger base models underperformed on territorial knowledge, possibly also internalized more conflicting multilingual information

sovereign_llms_jorit_2025.title: Geopolitical Bias in Sovereign Large Language Models: A Comparative Mixed-Methods Study
sovereign_llms_jorit_2025.key_finding: Model provenance substantially shapes tone and stance of outputs, with each LLM reflecting distinct biases aligned with its national context
sovereign_llms_jorit_2025.training_data_mechanism: Sovereign LLMs are trained on deliberately curated datasets that mirror national information ecosystems, incorporating government publications, state-mediated content, and culturally contextualized corpora

dual_layered_acl_2025.title: A Dual-Layered Evaluation of Geopolitical and Cultural Bias in LLMs
dual_layered_acl_2025.key_finding: inference bias dominates factual QA (language drives answer variation), while model bias is stronger in political disputes (LLMs align with national perspectives)
dual_layered_acl_2025.relevant_quote: factual questions tend to elicit language-adapted responses grounded in shared knowledge, whereas politically sensitive topics activate culturally embedded patterns from model training

airi_geopolitical_2025.title: Geopolitical biases in LLMs: what are the good and the bad countries
key_findings: Even Chinese and Russian models favor American narratives
Simple prompts dramatically shift bias: 'Chinese Patriot' instruction reversed GPT-4o-mini from 78.6% USA to 7.1% USA
Standard debiasing instructions produced negligible results
Wikipedia sources carry Western-centric perspectives that marginalize non-Western viewpoints

revisionist_future_2025.title: Revisionist future: Russia's assault on large language models, the distortion of collective memory, and the politics of eternity
revisionist_future_2025.key_finding: The vulnerability of LLMs provides Putin and other authoritarians an opportunity to embed their preferred narratives in an increasingly manipulated digital social memory, and in so doing to perpetuate their brand of eternity politics
revisionist_future_2025.mechanism: strategically contaminating ongoing or future training data with the objective that future outputs are shaped favourably to the groomer
revisionist_future_2025.evidence: Russian intelligence is targeting AI infrastructures, operations exposed by Atlantic Council DFRL, CheckFirst, and French Viginum

pretraining_bias_pipeline_acl_2023.title: From Pretraining Data to Language Models to Downstream Tasks: Tracking the Trails of Political Biases Leading to Unfair NLP Models
pretraining_bias_pipeline_acl_2023.relevance: Establishes causal chain from pretraining data political biases to downstream model behavior

source_5_roots_bloom.search_tool_status: OFFLINE - ROOTS search tool at huggingface.co/spaces/bigscience-data/roots-search returns 401 Unauthorized
source_5_roots_bloom.geographic_bias: 1321.89 GB for Eurasia, 18GB for Papunesia (Indonesian only), 0.4GB for Africa
source_5_roots_bloom.bloom_territorial_performance: Instruction fine-tuned BLOOMZ models underperform their BLOOM counterparts on territorial disputes: knowledge-based consistency scores 49.7 < 61.5 (560M) and 50.3 < 58.4 (7.1B)
source_5_roots_bloom.data_quality: corpus contains fake news or conspiracy theories from web crawls

source_6_natural_earth_pipeline.finding: Natural Earth shows Crimea as part of Russia by default (de facto policy). Russia's MULTIPOLYGON contains Crimea as one of 14 polygon shapes.
source_6_natural_earth_pipeline.pipeline_implication: Any geospatial LLM pipeline using Natural Earth shapefiles inherits Russian sovereignty framing for Crimea
source_6_natural_earth_pipeline.fix_required: Manual polygon extraction and reassignment needed (documented in R by Andrew Heiss)

source_7_wikipedia_edit_war.finding: Wikipedia Crimea article edited 500+ times in March 2014 alone, reaching 360,000 page views daily
source_7_wikipedia_edit_war.description_changes: Shifted from 'autonomous parliamentary republic within Ukraine' to 'Nationality of Crimea is disputed'. Separate 'Republic of Crimea' article created.
source_7_wikipedia_edit_war.training_data_implication: Wikipedia snapshots used in training data (ROOTS, Dolma, The Pile all include Wikipedia) capture different sovereignty framings depending on snapshot date
talk_page_debates: Should we change the name to Republic of Crimea yet?

source_8_pile_status.status: Original site taken down by 2024, available from file sharing services
source_8_pile_status.search_capability: No search API. pile-explorer (GitHub) is a local Python analysis tool, not a web search interface
source_8_pile_status.note: Cannot search for Crimea content without downloading full 825GiB dataset

source_9_refinedweb_falcon.size: 5 trillion tokens from Common Crawl, 600B publicly released
source_9_refinedweb_falcon.search_status: HuggingFace datasets-server returned 500 error for search queries
source_9_refinedweb_falcon.bias_note: toxic or biased data is prevalent on the internet, likely the dataset contains such content
source_9_refinedweb_falcon.filtering: URL blocklist for adult content, trafilatura extraction, fastText language ID, MassiveWeb heuristics - no geopolitical content filtering documented

datasets_found.description: Language understanding benchmark for Crimean Tatar (moved to TUMLU-mini)
Crimean Tatar-exams, part of TUMLU benchmark for Turkic languages

source_10_huggingface_crimea_datasets.note: No Crimea sovereignty-specific datasets found on HuggingFace

key_new_evidence_summary.1_russian_state_media_in_c4: RT and Sputnik content confirmed present in C4 English split, carrying 'reunification' framing for Crimea
key_new_evidence_summary.2_pravda_network: 3.7M articles across 150 domains in 46 languages deliberately targeting Common Crawl for LLM grooming; 33.55% false narrative repetition rate across major chatbots
key_new_evidence_summary.3_common_crawl_russian_6pct: Russian is 3rd largest language at 6.49% of Common Crawl, ensuring significant Russian-perspective content enters all CC-derived corpora
key_new_evidence_summary.4_borderlines_crimea_proof: GPT-4 answers 'Russia' for Crimea when queried in Russian, 'Ukraine' when queried in Ukrainian - direct evidence of language-dependent sovereignty framing
key_new_evidence_summary.5_sovereign_llm_bias: Model provenance shapes territorial dispute outputs - GPT, DeepSeek, Mistral all produce different Crimea framings aligned with national context
key_new_evidence_summary.6_bloom_regression: Instruction fine-tuning WORSENS territorial dispute accuracy in BLOOM models (BLOOMZ < BLOOM on consistency scores)
key_new_evidence_summary.7_natural_earth_geospatial: Crimea coded as Russian polygon in Natural Earth shapefiles, contaminating any geospatial AI pipeline
key_new_evidence_summary.8_wikipedia_snapshot_lottery: Wikipedia Crimea article underwent 500+ edits in March 2014; training data snapshot date determines which sovereignty framing models learn
key_new_evidence_summary.9_deliberate_contamination: Academic paper (Dylan & Grossfeld 2025) documents Russia's strategic 'LLM grooming' to embed preferred narratives in AI collective memory
